name: Japanese N2 Grammar
description: Around 200 JLPT N2 grammar patterns. Conjunctions, conditionals, nuanced stance markers. Fill in the blank.

facts.quizQuestion:
そのような事故は十分起こり{___}。
(Such an accident is entirely possible.)
facts.explanation: 得る (can/possible — formal) — そのような事故は十分起こり得る。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sono you na jiko ha juubun okori ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Such an accident is entirely possible.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 得る — can/possible — formal

facts.quizQuestion:
努力すれば何でも成し{___}。
(If you make an effort, you can achieve anything.)
facts.explanation: 得る (can/possible) — 努力すれば何でも成し得る。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: doryoku sure ba nani demo nashi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you make an effort, you can achieve anything.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 得る — can/possible

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな話はあり{___}ことだ。
(Such a story is entirely possible.)
facts.explanation: 得る (can/possible) — こんな話はあり得ることだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: konna hanashi ha ari ___ koto da
facts.sentenceTranslation: Such a story is entirely possible.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 得る — can/possible

facts.quizQuestion:
この技術は多くの問題を解決し{___}可能性がある。
(This technology has the possibility of being able to solve many problems.)
facts.explanation: 得る (can/possible) — この技術は多くの問題を解決し得る可能性がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono gijutsu ha ooku no mondai wo kaiketsu shi ___ kanou sei ga aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: This technology has the possibility of being able to solve many problems.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 得る — can/possible

facts.quizQuestion:
連絡先を知らないから知らせ{___}。
(I don't know their contact information so there is no way to notify them.)
facts.explanation: ようがない (no way to/there is no means) — 連絡先を知らないから知らせようがない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: renraku saki wo shira nai kara shirase ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't know their contact information so there is no way to notify them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ようがない — no way to/there is no means

facts.quizQuestion:
証拠がないから証明し{___}。
(Without evidence, there is no way to prove it.)
facts.explanation: ようがない (no way to) — 証拠がないから証明しようがない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: shouko ga nai kara shoumei shi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without evidence, there is no way to prove it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ようがない — no way to

facts.quizQuestion:
こんなに複雑な問題は説明し{___}。
(Such a complicated problem — there is just no way to explain it.)
facts.explanation: ようがない (no way to) — こんなに複雑な問題は説明しようがない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: konnani fukuzatsu na mondai ha setsumei shi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Such a complicated problem — there is just no way to explain it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ようがない — no way to

facts.quizQuestion:
道具がないと修理し{___}。
(Without tools, there is no way to repair it.)
facts.explanation: ようがない (no way to) — 道具がないと修理しようがない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: dougu ga nai to shuuri shi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without tools, there is no way to repair it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ようがない — no way to

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は英語{___}、中国語も話せる。
(Not only English but also Chinese — he can speak it.)
facts.explanation: ばかりか (not only but also) — 彼は英語ばかりか、中国語も話せる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha eigo ___ , chuugoku go mo hanaseru
facts.sentenceTranslation: Not only English but also Chinese — he can speak it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかりか — not only but also

facts.quizQuestion:
この問題は日本{___}、世界中で議論されている。
(This issue is not only discussed in Japan but around the whole world.)
facts.explanation: ばかりか (not only but also) — この問題は日本ばかりか、世界中で議論されている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono mondai ha nippon ___ , sekaijuu de giron sa re te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: This issue is not only discussed in Japan but around the whole world.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかりか — not only but also

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は歌が上手な{___}、ダンスも素晴らしい。
(Not only is he good at singing, but his dancing is also wonderful.)
facts.explanation: ばかりか (not only but also) — 彼は歌が上手なばかりか、ダンスも素晴らしい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha uta ga jouzu na ___ , dansu mo subarashii
facts.sentenceTranslation: Not only is he good at singing, but his dancing is also wonderful.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかりか — not only but also

facts.quizQuestion:
この店は安い{___}、品質も高い。
(Not only is this store cheap, but the quality is also high.)
facts.explanation: ばかりか (not only but also) — この店は安いばかりか、品質も高い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: quality (of a product or a service)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kono mise ha yasui ___ , hinshitsu mo takai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Not only is this store cheap, but the quality is also high.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかりか — not only but also

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は困難に負ける{___}、夢を追い続けた。
(Without being defeated by difficulties, she continued to pursue her dream.)
facts.explanation: ことなく (without doing) — 彼女は困難に負けることなく、夢を追い続けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha konnan ni makeru ___ , yume wo oi tsuzuke ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without being defeated by difficulties, she continued to pursue her dream.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことなく — without doing

facts.quizQuestion:
この機械は一度も故障する{___}、10年間動き続けた。
(This machine continued to run for 10 years without breaking down even once.)
facts.explanation: ことなく (without doing) — この機械は一度も故障することなく、10年間動き続けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono kikai ha ichi do mo koshou suru ___ , 10 nenkan ugoki tsuzuke ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: This machine continued to run for 10 years without breaking down even once.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことなく — without doing

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は誰にも知られる{___}、密かに準備を進めた。
(Without anyone knowing, he quietly made preparations.)
facts.explanation: ことなく (without) — 彼は誰にも知られることなく、密かに準備を進めた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha dare ni mo shira reru ___ , hisoka ni junbi wo susume ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without anyone knowing, he quietly made preparations.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことなく — without

facts.quizQuestion:
休む{___}、毎日働き続けた。
(Without resting, he continued to work every day.)
facts.explanation: ことなく (without) — 休むことなく、毎日働き続けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: yasumu ___ , mainichi hataraki tsuzuke ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without resting, he continued to work every day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことなく — without

facts.quizQuestion:
語学力{___}、コミュニケーション能力も必要だ。
(In addition to language ability, communication skills are also necessary.)
facts.explanation: に加えて (in addition to) — 語学力に加えて、コミュニケーション能力も必要だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: gogaku ryoku ___ , komyunikeeshon nouryoku mo hitsuyou da
facts.sentenceTranslation: In addition to language ability, communication skills are also necessary.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に加えて — in addition to

facts.quizQuestion:
悪天候{___}、道路工事もあり、渋滞がひどかった。
(In addition to bad weather, there was also road construction, causing terrible traffic jams.)
facts.explanation: に加えて (in addition to) — 悪天候に加えて、道路工事もあり、渋滞がひどかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: akutenkou ___ , douro kouji mo ari, juutai ga hidoka ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: In addition to bad weather, there was also road construction, causing terrible traffic jams.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に加えて — in addition to

facts.quizQuestion:
既存の製品ラインナップ{___}、新しい商品を発売した。
(In addition to the existing product lineup, they launched a new product.)
facts.explanation: に加えて (in addition to) — 既存の製品ラインナップに加えて、新しい商品を発売した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kison no seihin rainnappu ___ , atarashii shouhin wo hatsubai shi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: In addition to the existing product lineup, they launched a new product.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に加えて — in addition to

facts.quizQuestion:
高い技術力{___}、豊富な経験があれば、就職は有利だ。
(In addition to high technical skills, having abundant experience is advantageous for job hunting.)
facts.explanation: に加えて (in addition to) — 高い技術力に加えて、豊富な経験があれば、就職は有利だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: takai gijutsu ryoku ___ , houfu na keiken ga are ba, shuushoku ha yuuri da
facts.sentenceTranslation: In addition to high technical skills, having abundant experience is advantageous for job hunting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に加えて — in addition to

facts.quizQuestion:
財布を忘れ、{___}電車も乗り遅れてしまった。
(I forgot my wallet, and on top of that, I missed the train.)
facts.explanation: おまけに (in addition/on top of that) — 財布を忘れ、おまけに電車も乗り遅れてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: saifu wo wasure, ___ densha mo noriokure te shima ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I forgot my wallet, and on top of that, I missed the train.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おまけに — in addition/on top of that

facts.quizQuestion:
今日は遅刻して、{___}大事な書類も忘れた。
(Today I was late, and on top of that, I also forgot an important document.)
facts.explanation: おまけに (on top of that/additionally — often negative) — 今日は遅刻して、おまけに大事な書類も忘れた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyou ha chikoku shi te, ___ daiji na shorui mo wasure ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today I was late, and on top of that, I also forgot an important document.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おまけに — on top of that/additionally — often negative

facts.quizQuestion:
雨に濡れてしまい、{___}風邪まで引いた。
(I got soaked in the rain, and on top of that, I even caught a cold.)
facts.explanation: おまけに (on top of that) — 雨に濡れてしまい、おまけに風邪まで引いた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ame ni nure te shimai, ___ kaze made hii ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I got soaked in the rain, and on top of that, I even caught a cold.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おまけに — on top of that

facts.quizQuestion:
料理が美味しく、{___}値段も安い。
(The food is delicious, and on top of that, the prices are cheap.)
facts.explanation: おまけに (on top of that — can also be positive) — 料理が美味しく、おまけに値段も安い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ryouri ga oishiku, ___ nedan mo yasui
facts.sentenceTranslation: The food is delicious, and on top of that, the prices are cheap.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おまけに — on top of that — can also be positive

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は頭がいい{___}、スポーツも得意だ。
(She is smart, and on top of that, she is good at sports.)
facts.explanation: 上に (as well as/besides/on top of that) — 彼女は頭がいい上に、スポーツも得意だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha atama ga ii ___ , supootsu mo tokui da
facts.sentenceTranslation: She is smart, and on top of that, she is good at sports.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 上に — as well as/besides/on top of that

facts.quizQuestion:
この仕事は時間がかかる{___}、体力も必要だ。
(This work takes time, and on top of that, physical strength is also necessary.)
facts.explanation: 上に (as well as/besides) — この仕事は時間がかかる上に、体力も必要だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to take (a resource, e.g. time or money)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kono shigoto ha jikan ga kakaru ___ , tairyoku mo hitsuyou da
facts.sentenceTranslation: This work takes time, and on top of that, physical strength is also necessary.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 上に — as well as/besides

facts.quizQuestion:
今日は雨が降っている{___}、風も強い。
(Today it is raining, and on top of that, the wind is strong.)
facts.explanation: 上に (as well as/besides) — 今日は雨が降っている上に、風も強い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to fall (of rain, snow, ash, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kyou ha ame ga fu te iru ___ , kaze mo tsuyoi
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today it is raining, and on top of that, the wind is strong.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 上に — as well as/besides

facts.quizQuestion:
その店は値段が高い{___}、サービスも悪い。
(That restaurant is expensive, and on top of that, the service is also bad.)
facts.explanation: 上に (as well as/besides) — その店は値段が高い上に、サービスも悪い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sono mise ha nedan ga takai ___ , saabisu mo warui
facts.sentenceTranslation: That restaurant is expensive, and on top of that, the service is also bad.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 上に — as well as/besides

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな無茶な運転をすると、事故を起こし{___}よ。
(If you drive so recklessly, you might cause an accident.)
facts.explanation: かねない (might/could — negative possibility) — そんな無茶な運転をすると、事故を起こしかねないよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sonna mucha na unten wo suru to, jiko wo okoshi ___ yo
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you drive so recklessly, you might cause an accident.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かねない — might/could — negative possibility

facts.quizQuestion:
このままでは会社が倒産し{___}状況だ。
(At this rate, the company might go bankrupt.)
facts.explanation: かねない (might/could) — このままでは会社が倒産しかねない状況だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono mama de ha kaisha ga tousan shi ___ joukyou da
facts.sentenceTranslation: At this rate, the company might go bankrupt.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かねない — might/could

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の発言は人々を傷つけ{___}ので、注意が必要だ。
(His remarks could hurt people, so caution is necessary.)
facts.explanation: かねない (might/could) — 彼の発言は人々を傷つけかねないので、注意が必要だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no hatsugen ha hitobito wo kizutsuke ___ node, chuui ga hitsuyou da
facts.sentenceTranslation: His remarks could hurt people, so caution is necessary.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かねない — might/could

facts.quizQuestion:
無理をすると体を壊し{___}から、休んでください。
(If you push yourself too hard, you might break down, so please rest.)
facts.explanation: かねない (might/could) — 無理をすると体を壊しかねないから、休んでください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: muri wo suru to karada wo kowashi ___ kara, yasun de kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you push yourself too hard, you might break down, so please rest.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かねない — might/could

facts.quizQuestion:
最近の若者はSNSを使いすぎる{___}。
(Recent young people tend to use social media too much.)
facts.explanation: 傾向がある (tendency to) — 最近の若者はSNSを使いすぎる傾向がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: saikin no wakamono ha SNS wo tsukai sugiru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Recent young people tend to use social media too much.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 傾向がある — tendency to

facts.quizQuestion:
ストレスがたまると食べすぎる{___}のは私だけでしょうか。
(Am I the only one with a tendency to overeat when stressed?)
facts.explanation: 傾向がある (tendency to) — ストレスがたまると食べすぎる傾向があるのは私だけでしょうか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sutoresu ga tamaru to tabe sugiru ___ no ha watashi dake desho u ka
facts.sentenceTranslation: Am I the only one with a tendency to overeat when stressed?
facts.grammarPointLabel: 傾向がある — tendency to

facts.quizQuestion:
日本では少子化が進む{___}が見られる。
(In Japan, a tendency toward declining birthrate can be seen.)
facts.explanation: 傾向がある (tendency) — 日本では少子化が進む傾向がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: nippon de ha shoushika ga susumu ___ ga mi rareru
facts.sentenceTranslation: In Japan, a tendency toward declining birthrate can be seen.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 傾向がある — tendency

facts.quizQuestion:
この地域は夏に雨が少ない{___}が統計で示されている。
(Statistics show a tendency for less rainfall in this region in summer.)
facts.explanation: 傾向がある (tendency) — この地域は夏に雨が少ない傾向があることが統計で示されている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono chiiki ha natsu ni ame ga sukunai ___ ga toukei de shimesa re te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: Statistics show a tendency for less rainfall in this region in summer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 傾向がある — tendency

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女のピアノの演奏には、心を打つ{___}。
(There is something moving about her piano performance.)
facts.explanation: ものがある (there is something about it) — 彼女のピアノの演奏には、心を打つものがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo no piano no ensou ni ha, kokoro wo utsu ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is something moving about her piano performance.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものがある — there is something about it

facts.quizQuestion:
あの映画の結末には、考えさせられる{___}。
(There is something thought-provoking about that movie's ending.)
facts.explanation: ものがある (there is something about it) — あの映画の結末には、考えさせられるものがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ano eiga no ketsumatsu ni ha, kangae sase rareru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is something thought-provoking about that movie's ending.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものがある — there is something about it

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の言葉には、不思議と人をひきつける{___}。
(There is something strangely attractive about his words.)
facts.explanation: ものがある (there is something about it) — 彼の言葉には、不思議と人をひきつけるものがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no kotoba ni ha, fushigi to hito wo hikitsukeru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is something strangely attractive about his words.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものがある — there is something about it

facts.quizQuestion:
この絵画には見る人を圧倒する迫力{___}。
(This painting has something overwhelming about it that overpowers viewers.)
facts.explanation: ものがある (there is something about it) — この絵画には見る人を圧倒する迫力のものがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono kaiga ni ha miru hito wo attou suru hakuryoku ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: This painting has something overwhelming about it that overpowers viewers.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものがある — there is something about it

facts.quizQuestion:
台風が近づいているため、洪水が発生する{___}。
(As the typhoon approaches, there is a risk of flooding.)
facts.explanation: おそれがある (there is a risk/fear that) — 台風が近づいているため、洪水が発生するおそれがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: taifuu ga chikazui te iru tame, kouzui ga hassei suru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: As the typhoon approaches, there is a risk of flooding.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おそれがある — there is a risk/fear that

facts.quizQuestion:
この薬は副作用が出る{___}ので、注意してください。
(This medicine has a risk of side effects, so please be careful.)
facts.explanation: おそれがある (risk/fear that) — この薬は副作用が出るおそれがあるので、注意してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono kusuri ha fukusayou ga deru ___ node, chuui shi te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: This medicine has a risk of side effects, so please be careful.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おそれがある — risk/fear that

facts.quizQuestion:
個人情報が漏洩する{___}として、システムを停止した。
(The system was shut down due to the risk of personal information leaking.)
facts.explanation: おそれがある (risk that) — 個人情報が漏洩するおそれがあるとして、システムを停止した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kojin jouhou ga rousetsu suru ___ toshite, shisutemu wo teishi shi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: The system was shut down due to the risk of personal information leaking.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おそれがある — risk that

facts.quizQuestion:
大雪で交通が麻痺する{___}。外出は控えてください。
(There is a risk that traffic will be paralyzed by heavy snow. Please refrain from going out.)
facts.explanation: おそれがある (risk that) — 大雪で交通が麻痺するおそれがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ooyuki de koutsuu ga mahi suru ___ . gaishutsu ha hikae te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is a risk that traffic will be paralyzed by heavy snow. Please refrain from going out.
facts.grammarPointLabel: おそれがある — risk that

facts.quizQuestion:
この雨はやみ{___}。もう少し待ちましょう。
(This rain doesn't look like it will stop. Let's wait a little longer.)
facts.explanation: そうにない (unlikely to) — この雨はやみそうにない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono ame ha yami ___ . mousukoshi machi masho u
facts.sentenceTranslation: This rain doesn't look like it will stop. Let's wait a little longer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: そうにない — unlikely to

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は試験に合格し{___}。全然勉強していないから。
(He doesn't look likely to pass the exam. Because he hasn't studied at all.)
facts.explanation: そうにない (unlikely to) — 彼は試験に合格しそうにない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha shiken ni goukaku shi ___ . zenzen benkyou shi te i nai kara
facts.sentenceTranslation: He doesn't look likely to pass the exam. Because he hasn't studied at all.
facts.grammarPointLabel: そうにない — unlikely to

facts.quizQuestion:
この仕事は今日中に終わり{___}ね。明日も残業だ。
(This work doesn't look like it will finish today. We'll be working overtime tomorrow too.)

(It's not that I dislike him, but it's true I don't want to work with him.)
facts.explanation: というわけではない (not that/doesn't mean) — 嫌いだというわけではないが partially denies a strong statement. 彼が嫌いだというわけではないが、一緒に仕事したくないのは本当だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.pronunciation: to iu wake de wa nai
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga kirai da ___ ga, issho ni shigoto shi taku nai no ha hontou da
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's not that I dislike him, but it's true I don't want to work with him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: というわけではない — not that/doesn't mean

facts.quizQuestion:
お金持ちだから幸せだ{___}。
(Being rich doesn't necessarily mean being happy.)
facts.explanation: というわけではない (not necessarily) — お金持ちだから幸せだというわけではない negates an assumed cause-effect. お金持ちだから幸せだというわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.pronunciation: to iu wake de wa nai
facts.sentenceRomaji: o kanemochi da kara shiawase da ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Being rich doesn't necessarily mean being happy.
facts.grammarPointLabel: というわけではない — not necessarily

facts.quizQuestion:
勉強すれば必ず合格できる{___}。
(Studying doesn't necessarily guarantee you will pass.)
facts.explanation: というわけではない (not necessarily) — 勉強すれば必ず合格できるというわけではない denies a guaranteed outcome. 勉強すれば必ず合格できるというわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.pronunciation: to iu wake de wa nai
facts.sentenceRomaji: benkyou sure ba kanarazu goukaku dekiru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Studying doesn't necessarily guarantee you will pass.
facts.grammarPointLabel: というわけではない — not necessarily

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は10年間日本に住んでいたから、日本語が上手な{___}。
(He lived in Japan for 10 years, so that's why his Japanese is good.)
facts.explanation: わけだ (that's why/it makes sense that) — 10年住んでいたから上手なわけだ explains a natural consequence. 彼は10年間日本に住んでいたから、日本語が上手なわけだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha 10 nenkan nippon ni sun de i ta kara, nihongo ga jouzu na ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: He lived in Japan for 10 years, so that's why his Japanese is good.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけだ — that's why/it makes sense that

facts.quizQuestion:
昨日徹夜したから、今日眠い{___}。
(I stayed up all night yesterday, so that's why I'm sleepy today.)
facts.explanation: わけだ (that's why — logical conclusion) — 徹夜したから眠いわけだ presents a logical reason. 昨日徹夜したから、今日眠いわけだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kinou tetsuya shi ta kara, kyou nemui ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I stayed up all night yesterday, so that's why I'm sleepy today.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけだ — that's why — logical conclusion

facts.quizQuestion:
3×5は15だから、15個買えば3人で5個ずつ分けられる{___}。
(3×5 is 15, so that means buying 15 gives 5 each to 3 people.)
facts.explanation: わけだ (that means/it follows that) — mathematical or logical conclusion drawn with わけだ. 3×5は15だから、15個買えば3人で5個ずつ分けられるわけだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: 3kakeru 5 ha 15 da kara, 15 ko kae ba 3 nin de 5 ko zutsu wake rareru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: 3×5 is 15, so that means buying 15 gives 5 each to 3 people.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけだ — that means/it follows that

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は双子だから、二人は顔がそっくりな{___}。
(She is a twin, so that's why the two look identical.)
facts.explanation: わけだ (that's why/it makes sense) — 双子だからそっくりなわけだ presents the reason as a natural explanation. 彼女は双子だから、二人は顔がそっくりなわけだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha futago da kara, ni nin ha kao ga sokkuri na ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: She is a twin, so that's why the two look identical.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけだ — that's why/it makes sense

facts.quizQuestion:
日本語が話せないから日本が嫌いな{___}。
(It's not that I dislike Japan just because I can't speak Japanese.)
facts.explanation: わけではない (not that/it doesn't mean) — 日本語が話せないから日本が嫌いなわけではない clarifies a misconception. 日本語が話せないから日本が嫌いなわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: nihongo ga hanase nai kara nippon ga kirai na ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's not that I dislike Japan just because I can't speak Japanese.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけではない — not that/it doesn't mean

facts.quizQuestion:
毎日運動しているから、特別健康な{___}。
(It's not that I'm especially healthy just because I exercise every day.)
facts.explanation: わけではない (it doesn't follow that) — 毎日運動しているから特別健康なわけではない negates a logical assumption. 毎日運動しているから、特別健康なわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: mainichi undou shi te iru kara, tokubetsu kenkou na ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's not that I'm especially healthy just because I exercise every day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけではない — it doesn't follow that

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が正しくない{___}が、私も完全に正しいとは言えない。
(It's not that he is wrong, but I can't say I'm completely right either.)
facts.explanation: わけではない (not that/partial negation) — 正しくないわけではないが qualifies a statement without full negation. 彼が正しくないわけではないが、私も完全に正しいとは言えない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga tadashiku nai ___ ga, watashi mo kanzen ni tadashii to ha ie nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's not that he is wrong, but I can't say I'm completely right either.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけではない — not that/partial negation

facts.quizQuestion:
仕事が忙しいから、全く休めない{___}。
(It's not that I can't rest at all just because work is busy.)
facts.explanation: わけではない (not necessarily) — 忙しいから全く休めないわけではない refutes an extreme conclusion. 仕事が忙しいから、全く休めないわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: shigoto ga isogashii kara, mattaku yasume nai ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's not that I can't rest at all just because work is busy.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わけではない — not necessarily

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は先月から{___}、連絡がない。
(Since last month, there's been no contact from him.)
facts.explanation: きり (since/only — with negative) — 先月から会ったきり means since meeting last month (and not since). 彼は先月から会ったきり、連絡がない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha sengetsu kara ___ , renraku ga nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since last month, there's been no contact from him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: きり — since/only — with negative

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は一度そう言った{___}、それ以後は何も言わなかった。
(She said that once, and after that said nothing more.)
facts.explanation: きり (since/only once) — 一度言ったきり expresses that after doing something once, no further action follows. 彼女は一度そう言ったきり、それ以後は何も言わなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha ichido sou i ta ___ , sore igo ha nani mo iwa naka ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: She said that once, and after that said nothing more.
facts.grammarPointLabel: きり — since/only once

facts.quizQuestion:
部屋にはあなた一人{___}だ。
(There's only you alone in the room.)
facts.explanation: きり (only) — 一人きり expresses that there is only that one person. 部屋にはあなた一人きりだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: heya ni ha anata ichi nin ___ da
facts.sentenceTranslation: There's only you alone in the room.
facts.grammarPointLabel: きり — only

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は家を出た{___}、まだ帰ってこない。
(Since he left the house, he still hasn't come back.)
facts.explanation: きり (since — with negative result) — 出たきり with a negative indicates that since leaving, there has been no return. 彼は家を出たきり、まだ帰ってこない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha ie wo de ta ___ , mada kae te ko nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since he left the house, he still hasn't come back.
facts.grammarPointLabel: きり — since — with negative result

facts.quizQuestion:
今日は会議を{___}、直接作業に取り掛かろう。
(Today let's get straight to work, without a meeting.)
facts.explanation: 抜きで (without/excluding) — 会議を抜きで means excluding/without the meeting. 今日は会議を抜きで、直接作業に取り掛かろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyou ha kaigi wo ___ , chokusetsu sagyou ni torikakaro u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Today let's get straight to work, without a meeting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 抜きで — without/excluding

facts.quizQuestion:
冗談{___}、本当のことを話してください。
(Setting jokes aside, please tell me the truth.)
facts.explanation: 抜きで (without/setting aside) — 冗談抜きで means seriously/without joking. 冗談抜きで、本当のことを話してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: joudan ___ , hontou no koto wo hanashi te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Setting jokes aside, please tell me the truth.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 抜きで — without/setting aside

facts.quizQuestion:
前置き{___}、結論から話しましょう。
(Without any preamble, let's talk from the conclusion.)
facts.explanation: 抜きで (without/excluding) — 前置き抜きで means without preamble/getting straight to the point. 前置き抜きで、結論から話しましょう。

sentenceFurigana.g: conclusion (of an argument, discussion, study, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: maeoki ___ , ketsuron kara hanashi masho u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without any preamble, let's talk from the conclusion.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 抜きで — without/excluding

facts.quizQuestion:
お世辞{___}、あなたの料理は本当においしい。

facts.explanation: 抜きで (without/setting aside) — お世辞抜きで means without flattery/sincerely. お世辞抜きで、あなたの料理は本当においしい。

facts.sentenceRomaji: o seji ___ , anata no ryouri ha hontouni oishii

facts.quizQuestion:
どんなに辛くても、最後まで{___}つもりだ。

facts.explanation: 抜く (do completely/to the end) — やり抜く means to carry out completely. どんなに辛くても、最後までやり抜くつもりだ。

facts.grammarPointLabel: 抜く — do completely/to the end

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はマラソンの全コースを{___}。

facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha marason no zen koosu wo ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: He ran through the entire marathon course.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は難しい専門書を一週間で{___}。

facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha muzukashii senmon sho wo ichi shuukan de ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: She read through the difficult technical book in one week.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 抜く — do completely

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は戦争という困難な時代を{___}。
(He survived completely through the difficult wartime era.)
facts.explanation: 抜く (do completely/survive) — 生き抜く means to live through/survive entirely. 彼は戦争という困難な時代を生き抜いた。

facts.quizQuestion:
旅行に行きたかったが、結局行か{___}だった。

facts.explanation: ずじまい (ended up never doing) — 行かずじまい means ended up not going. 旅行に行きたかったが、結局行かずじまいだった。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ni ayamaro u to omo te i ta ga, ie ___ da ta

facts.grammarPointLabel: ずじまい — ended up never doing

facts.quizQuestion:
せっかく買ったピアノだが、練習せ{___}に終わった。

facts.quizQuestion:
勉強すれ{___}するほど、成績が上がる。
(The more you study, the better your grades get.)
facts.explanation: ば～ほど (the more X, the more Y) — 勉強すればするほど成績が上がる is the classic ば～ほど pattern. 勉強すればするほど、成績が上がる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: benkyou sure ___ suru hodo, seiseki ga agaru

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: kangae re ___ kangaeru hodo, kotae ga wakara naku naru
facts.sentenceTranslation: The more I think about it, the less I understand the answer.

練習すれ{___}するほど、上手になる。

facts.sentenceRomaji: renshuu sure ___ suru hodo, jouzu ni naru
facts.sentenceTranslation: The more you practice, the more skilled you become.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ば～ほど — the more...the more

facts.quizQuestion:
知れ{___}知るほど、日本語は奥深い。

facts.explanation: ば～ほど (the more...the more) — 知れば知るほど奥深い describes increasing appreciation. 知れば知るほど、日本語は奥深い。

facts.sentenceRomaji: shire ___ shiru hodo, nihongo ha okufukai
facts.sentenceTranslation: The more you learn about it, the more profound Japanese is.

facts.quizQuestion:

(Without caring about the time, he worked until midnight.)

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
一度の失敗で落ち込む{___}。

facts.quizQuestion:
あんな人の言葉を気にする{___}。

facts.quizQuestion:
わざわざ謝りに行く{___}、メールで十分だ。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: chikaku no konbini made, kuruma de iku ___

facts.grammarPointLabel: までもない — no need to go so far as

facts.sentenceRomaji: iu ___ , kare ha nippon de mo yuumei na kagaku sha da

facts.quizQuestion:
これほど明らかな事実を証明する{___}。

facts.quizQuestion:
タクシーを使えば、雨に濡れ{___}。
(If you take a taxi, you can get by without getting wet in the rain.)
facts.explanation: ないですむ (get by without doing/avoid the need to) — 濡れないですむ means to avoid getting wet. タクシーを使えば、雨に濡れないですむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: takushii wo tsukae ba, ame ni nure ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you take a taxi, you can get by without getting wet in the rain.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないですむ — get by without doing/avoid the need to

facts.quizQuestion:
早めに予約しておけば、並ば{___}。
(If you book in advance, you can get by without having to queue.)
facts.explanation: ないですむ (get by without — avoid necessity) — 並ばないですむ means to avoid having to wait in line. 早めに予約しておけば、並ばないですむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: hayame ni yoyaku shi te oke ba, naraba ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you book in advance, you can get by without having to queue.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないですむ — get by without — avoid necessity

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が手伝ってくれたおかげで、残業し{___}。
(Thanks to his help, I managed to get by without working overtime.)
facts.explanation: ないですむ (get by without) — 残業しないですむ means to avoid the need to work overtime. 彼が手伝ってくれたおかげで、残業しないですむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga tetsuda te kure ta okage de, zangyou shi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Thanks to his help, I managed to get by without working overtime.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないですむ — get by without

facts.quizQuestion:
上手く説明できれば、誤解され{___}。
(If you can explain it well, you can get by without being misunderstood.)
facts.explanation: ないですむ (get by without) — 誤解されないですむ means to avoid being misunderstood. 上手く説明できれば、誤解されないですむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: umaku setsumei dekire ba, gokai sa re ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you can explain it well, you can get by without being misunderstood.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないですむ — get by without

facts.quizQuestion:
祖父が亡くなった知らせを聞いて、悲しく{___}。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sofu ga nakuna ta shirase wo kii te, kanashiku ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: On hearing the news of my grandfather's passing, I cannot help feeling sad.

facts.quizQuestion:
子供の頃の思い出が懐かしく{___}。
(I cannot help feeling nostalgic about my childhood memories.)
facts.explanation: てならない (cannot help feeling — deeper inner emotion) — 懐かしくてならない conveys deep involuntary nostalgia. 子供の頃の思い出が懐かしくてならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kodomo no koro no omoide ga natsukashiku ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I cannot help feeling nostalgic about my childhood memories.
facts.grammarPointLabel: てならない — cannot help feeling — deeper inner emotion

facts.quizQuestion:
あの時もっと頑張ればよかったと、後悔されて{___}。
(I cannot help feeling regret that I should have worked harder back then.)
facts.explanation: てならない (cannot help feeling) — 後悔されてならない expresses unavoidable regret welling up. あの時もっと頑張ればよかったと、後悔されてならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ano toki motto ganbare ba yoka ta to, koukai sa re te ___

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の努力が報われて、嬉しく{___}。
(I cannot help feeling happy that his efforts were rewarded.)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no doryoku ga mukuwa re te, ureshiku ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I cannot help feeling happy that his efforts were rewarded.
facts.grammarPointLabel: てならない — cannot help feeling — inner emotion

眠く{___}、授業中に何度も居眠りしてしまった。

facts.explanation: てしょうがない (can't help it/unbearably — colloquial) — 眠くてしょうがない is everyday colloquial for overwhelming sleepiness. 眠くてしょうがない、授業中に何度も居眠りしてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: nemuku ___ , jugyou chuu ni nan do mo inemuri shi te shima ta

彼の言い方が腹立たしく{___}。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no iikata ga haradatashiku ___

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女のことが心配{___}、毎日連絡を取っている。
(I'm so unbearably worried about her that I contact her every day.)
facts.explanation: てしょうがない (can't help it/unbearably — colloquial) — 心配でしょうがない means worried beyond control. 彼女のことが心配でしょうがない、毎日連絡を取っている。

facts.quizQuestion:
夏の暑さが嫌{___}。
(I find the summer heat unbearably unpleasant.)
facts.explanation: てたまらない (unbearably/to an extreme degree — involuntary feeling) — 嫌でたまらない expresses that the feeling is overwhelming. 夏の暑さが嫌でたまらない。

facts.quizQuestion:
試験の結果が気になっ{___}。
(I'm unbearably anxious about the exam results.)
facts.explanation: てたまらない (unbearably — strong involuntary feeling) — 気になってたまらない expresses extreme worry. 試験の結果が気になってたまらない。

facts.sentenceRomaji: shiken no kekka ga ki ni na ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm unbearably anxious about the exam results.

facts.quizQuestion:
空腹{___}、何か食べないといられない。
(I'm unbearably hungry and can't help but eat something.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kuufuku ___ , nani ka tabe nai to i rare nai

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女に会いたく{___}、毎日連絡してしまう。

facts.quizQuestion:

synonymGroups.reason: ないではいられない and ずにはいられない both mean 'cannot help but do' or 'cannot stop oneself from doing'. ずにはいられない uses the literary ず negative form.
All four patterns mean 'there is no choice but to' / 'the only option is to'. しかない and ほかない are the most common; より仕方がない and より他ない are slightly more formal.
に過ぎない and でしかない both mean 'is merely / is nothing more than'. でしかない carries a slightly stronger nuance of insufficiency.
にもかかわらず means 'despite / in spite of' (concessive). にかかわらず means 'regardless of / irrespective of'. They overlap in meaning and are frequently confused by learners.
を通じて and を通して are largely interchangeable and both mean 'through / via / by means of / throughout'. を通じて is slightly more common in written/formal contexts.
に基づいて and を元に both mean 'based on'. に基づいて is more formal and common in official/legal contexts; を元に is slightly more casual.
てたまらない, てならない, and てしょうがない all express an overwhelming feeling — 'extremely / unbearably / can't help but feel'. てならない is slightly more formal; てしょうがない is colloquial.
